content: Functional Core, Imperative Shell (FCIS) is an established architecture pattern where pure functions form the core (no side effects, same input always same output) and an imperative shell handles all I/O and effects. The core cannot call the shell; the shell orchestrates inputs flowing into the core and effects flowing out. Directly maps to Mandelbrut's Synthesis (pure, deterministic DayVector+Seed+Genome→Scene) and Paint (scene→pixels) layers. Testing the core requires zero mocks. The shell is the Devvit wiring layer.
